_about.purpose: Chronicle campaign schema v4. A generic, reusable schema for any tabletop RPG campaign log. Separates mechanical facts from narrative content. Designed for use with an LLM-assisted intake and editing workflow.
_about.usage: Copy this file and replace the _examples sections with your campaign data. Run the DnDBeyond sync process for each player character to populate mechanics.ability_scores and mechanics.spells_known. Populate narrative blocks manually or with LLM assistance from session notes.
_about.version_notes: v4 introduces the mechanics/narrative/notes three-layer pattern, structured cross-references by ID, DnDBeyond integration via ddb_id, and immutability as a first-class constraint.
_about.migration: To migrate from an earlier schema, run the conversion script which will distribute existing notes fields into mechanics and narrative blocks, flag ambiguous content for manual review, and fetch DnDBeyond data for any character with a ddb_id set.

_design_principles.immutability: Store only campaign facts — things that require a deliberate in-game decision or event to change. Never store transient game state: current HP, used spell slots, active conditions, temporary buffs. If it resets on a long rest, it does not belong here.
_design_principles.mechanical_block: The mechanics object contains structured, queryable facts. Every field is a direct lookup — no parsing required. An LLM given a mechanics block can answer 'can this character do X' without inference or guesswork.
_design_principles.narrative_block: The narrative object contains prose for human readers and LLM voice context. It must not contradict the mechanics block. It may be generous and evocative but must not introduce new mechanical facts not found elsewhere in the schema.
_design_principles.notes_fallback: The top-level notes field on each object is a fallback for campaign-specific context that fits neither mechanics nor narrative. It should be 1-3 sentences maximum. If notes grows beyond that, a new structured field is probably warranted.
_design_principles.references: Cross-references use ID strings only. Never embed a copy of another object's data inside a different object. Reference by ID and resolve at read time. Example: a quest references an NPC via 'npc_ref: npc_011', never by copying the NPC object.
_design_principles.item_vs_character_spells: A spellbook item contains spells as a property of the item object. A wizard character has a separate spells_known list. These are never the same field. Having access to a spellbook (spellbooks_accessible) is distinct from knowing a spell (spells_known). A spell moves from the book to the character's list only when explicitly transcribed.
_design_principles.ddb_integration: Set ddb_id to the DnDBeyond character ID for any player character with a DnDBeyond sheet. The sync process fetches ability scores, spells, feats, proficiencies, and narrative traits from DnDBeyond and populates the mechanics and narrative blocks. ddb_synced records the date of last sync. Characters without DnDBeyond sheets set ddb_id to null and are populated manually.
_design_principles.null_vs_absent: Use null for fields that are known to be empty. Omit fields entirely only if they are genuinely not applicable to this object type. A null value signals 'we checked and there is nothing here'. An absent field signals 'this field does not apply to this object type'.
_design_principles.ruleset_compatibility: This campaign uses the WotC backwards compatibility framework combining 5e, 5e expanded, 5.5e (2024), and 5.5e expanded content. Where a rule or feature has been rewritten in 5.5e, the 5.5e version is authoritative. Where no 5.5e version exists, the original 5e text applies. All expanded content from both editions is available on the same basis. Class feature and invocation entries in mechanics.class_features reflect the edition in which they were gained or replaced — the "(invocation)" suffix marks 5.5e Warlock invocations that were class features in 5e.
_design_principles.inventory_scope: Chronicle tracks only story-significant items: magic items, quest items, and items with narrative weight (e.g. Ched's Map, Bessel's Spellbook). Mundane gear (armor, standard weapons, basic tools) is managed in DnDBeyond and does not require Chronicle inventory entries. The inventory_and_loot array is not a complete equipment list — it is a record of items that matter to the campaign narrative or mechanics.

_field_type_reference._note: Reference for every field name used across all object types. Shows allowed values where enumerated.

slot_fields.action: OBJECT or null — the character's Action for this turn. For death saves, use name: 'Death Save', type: 'death_save', res: 'hit' (success) or 'miss' (failure). notes records cumulative count.
slot_fields.bonus_action: OBJECT or null — the character's Bonus Action. Shape: _action_record
slot_fields.reaction: OBJECT or null — a Reaction taken this round. Shape: _action_record plus reaction_trigger: STRING.
slot_fields.movement: STRING or null — brief movement description if narratively significant. Omit routine movement.
slot_fields.object_interaction: STRING or null — free object interaction e.g. 'drew second weapon', 'opened door'
slot_fields.notes: STRING or null — slot-level annotation that does not fit any sub-record

_attack_record._note: Shape used inside action.attacks[] and bonus_action.attacks[]. Each attack or spell beam is independent.
_attack_record.name: STRING — exact weapon name or spell beam label as stated at the table
_attack_record.val: INTEGER or null — stated numeric outcome. Null if not provided. Never inferred.
_attack_record.val_type: ENUM val_type or null — what the number represents (damage, healing, etc.)
_attack_record.target: STRING or null — target name or description

_action_record._note: Shape used for action, bonus_action, and reaction sub-records.
_action_record.name: STRING — exact spell, ability, or action name as stated at the table
_action_record.res: ENUM slot_result or null — overall result for single-outcome actions. Null when attacks[] is populated.
_action_record.val: INTEGER or null — stated numeric outcome for single-outcome actions. Null if not provided.
_action_record.attacks: ARRAY of _attack_record or null — for multi-attack actions (Extra Attack, Nick) and multi-beam spells (Eldritch Blast at higher levels). Each element is independent.

initiative_fields._note: The initiative object lives at the combat encounter level, not the round level. It is populated once per combat and applies to all rounds. Player initiative values are recorded from their dice roll. Enemy initiative may be exact (DM shared it), relative (noted as 'after Ashton'), or unknown. All positions are optional — partial initiative data is valid.
initiative_fields.combat_id: cbt_NNN — the combat this initiative order belongs to

entries.actor_id: pc_NNN | enemy actor label string — e.g. 'pc_001' or 'Ogre' or 'Goblins'
entries.initiative_value: INTEGER or null — the actual dice roll result if known
entries.position: INTEGER or null — ordinal position in the initiative order (1 = goes first)
entries.relative_to: STRING or null — for relative positions: 'after pc_003', 'before Ogre'

enemy_actor_fields._note: Enemy actors are defined at the combat encounter level in enemies_roster. This is the cast list for the combat — who was present. Each actor is either an individual (one creature with its own turn) or a group (multiple identical creatures acting collectively). Groups track their count if known.

_individual.actor_id: STRING — short label used to reference this actor in round.enemy_turns[]. Use the bestiary name for groups, a descriptive label for individuals e.g. 'Ogre', 'Vespon Queen', 'Alpha Wolf'
_individual.mon_ref: mon_NNN or null — bestiary reference if this creature type has been catalogued
_individual.count: INTEGER or null — for groups: number of creatures at combat start if known. null if unknown.
_individual.count_remaining: INTEGER or null — for groups: number alive at end of logged rounds

enemy_turn_fields._note: enemy_turns replaces the old enemy[] array on rounds. Each entry represents one enemy actor's turn in the round. Groups use a single entry for all their actions collectively. Individuals get their own entry with full action/bonus_action/reaction structure mirroring the player slot shape. Legendary and lair actions are separate entries with their own type.

_enemy_turn.actor_id: STRING — matches an actor_id in enemies_roster
_enemy_turn.initiative_position: INTEGER or null — where in the round this turn falls, if known
_enemy_turn.action: OBJECT or null — same _action_record shape as player slots. For multi-attack, use attacks[].
_enemy_turn.bonus_action: OBJECT or null — same shape as action
_enemy_turn.reaction: OBJECT or null — same shape as action, plus reaction_trigger
_enemy_turn.movement: STRING or null — significant movement only
_enemy_turn.notes: STRING or null — for group turns: summary of collective actions e.g. '3 attacks across different targets'

_legendary_action_entry._note: Legendary actions fire between other combatants' turns. They are separate entries with type 'legendary_action' in the action.type field. The legendary_action_count field records how many legendary action points were spent.
_legendary_action_entry.initiative_position: INTEGER or null — after which turn this legendary action fired

action.name: STRING
action.legendary_action_cost: INTEGER — number of legendary action points spent

_lair_action_entry._note: Lair actions fire at a fixed initiative count (typically 20), not tied to a specific creature's turn.
_lair_action_entry.initiative_position: INTEGER — the fixed initiative count e.g. 20

_id_registry._note: Current highest IDs per type. Increment before assigning new entries.

party.name: Zragar

species_abilities: Breath Weapon (Fire, 15ft cone or 30ft line, Dex save DC 10, 1d10)
Damage Resistance (Fire)
Darkvision 60ft
Draconic Flight (unlocks at level 5)

feats.name: Circle Magic (Intelligence)
feats.notes: Spells with casting time of an action or 1+ minute use Intelligence as spellcasting ability.

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.

spells_always_prepared.name: Mage Armor
spells_always_prepared.notes: Cast once per long rest without a spell slot via Magic Initiate feat.

narrative.appearance: Six-foot-eight tower of golden scales, gangly limbs. Tail-less by birth. Eyes the colour of hammered gold. Moves with a scholar's stoop. Height 6'8", weight 275 lbs. No hair. Gold eyes. Golden scales.
narrative.personality: Uses polysyllabic words that convey the impression of erudition, because I am terrified of being misunderstood. I am painfully polite and try to make myself physically smaller to avoid conflict. I view every challenge through the lens of a storybook trope. I am frugal to a fault, but generous with knowledge.
narrative.ideals: Stories are the only true form of immortality. I don't just want to save the day; I want to perform a deed so clever, so spectacular, and so heroic that scribes will be forced to write it down.
narrative.bonds: Derwin 'Goli' Warmfire is the protagonist of my life's story, and I am the narrator who ensures he survives to the final chapter. I provide the strategy so he doesn't have to pay the price for his bravery.
narrative.flaws: I suffer from 'Narrative Causality Syndrome.' I genuinely believe the world operates like a fantasy novel. I assume the scary glowing rune is a puzzle meant to be solved, not a death trap, and I often underestimate the brutality of violence because I expect the 'heroes' to have plot armor.
narrative.voice: Scholar-adventurer. Frames everything as epic narrative. Calculates odds so others don't have to. Motto: 'I calculate the odds so he doesn't have to.'
narrative.first_impression: A large, anxious, extremely polite dragonborn who is either about to do something brilliant or something catastrophically naive.

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.

milestones.event: Transcribed 7 level-1 spells from Bessel's Spellbook
milestones.note: After recovering item_002 at Bagyers Farm, Goldie transcribed all 7 level-1 spells into his personal spellbook: Shield, Magic Missile, Mage Armor, Find Familiar, Absorb Elements, Detect Magic, Comprehend Languages.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

party.campaign_notes: Familiar: Index the Owl — named session_001. Expert in historical adventure chronicles. Carries Bessel's Spellbook (item_002) — transcribed all 7 level-1 spells after session_003. Levels 2-3 spells (Misty Step, Scorching Ray, Knock, Spider Climb, Fireball, Tiny Hut) remain in the book only. Immigrant family — fled a war in their homelands to settle in Riverbend. Father works as a caravan guard and is frequently away. Mother lives in the village.

party.name: Malachite

species_abilities: Celestial Resistance — Necrotic and Radiant damage resistance
Darkvision 60ft
Healing Hands — touch creature, roll Proficiency Bonus d4s, restore that many HP (once per long rest)
Light Bearer — knows Light cantrip, Charisma is spellcasting ability
Celestial Revelation (unlocks level 3) — once per long rest, Bonus Action transform: Heavenly Wings (fly speed), Inner Radiance (radiant aura), or Necrotic Shroud (frighten nearby enemies)

narrative.backstory: Raised in a giant community after being orphaned or separated from his family. When his Aasimar traits began manifesting — celestial light, unusual growth, supernatural presence — the giants sent him away. Ched Warmfire found him on the road during the original party's travels and brought him to Riverbend, knowing the town's Halfling-founded culture of hospitality would accept him. He and Asphodel (pc_004) arrived together and were raised in Riverbend.

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

features_gained: Vitality of the Tree (Path of the World Tree)
Branches of the Tree (Path of the World Tree)

party.campaign_notes: Aasimar Barbarian, Path of the World Tree. Male. Giant Foundling — raised in a giant community until his Aasimar traits manifested; cast out and found by Ched on the road. Brought to Riverbend by Ched, Blair, and Rufus alongside Asphodel (pc_004). Both Malachite and Asphodel are Aasimar — Riverbend's founding culture of hospitality made it the right place for them. Carries Boots of Striding and Springing (item_005), attuned.

party.name: Ashton

species_abilities: Darkvision 60ft
Otherworldly Presence — knows Thaumaturgy cantrip
Fiendish Legacy (Chthonic) — Necrotic resistance, Chill Touch cantrip
Fiendish Legacy spell at level 3: False Life (once per long rest without slot)

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

features_gained: Tough (feat via Lessons of the First Ones)

party.campaign_notes: Tiefling, Scion of Hadar — Great Old One warlock patron (2024 rules). Invocations: Pact of the Blade, Agonizing Blast (Eldritch Blast), Lessons of the First Ones (grants Tough feat). Carries Cloak of Protection (item_006), attuned. Destroyed the Bagyers Farm Pylon with Eldritch Blast — session_003. Daughter of the owners of the local general store in Riverbend.

party.name: Asphodel

species_abilities: Celestial Resistance — Necrotic and Radiant damage resistance
Darkvision 60ft
Healing Hands — touch creature, roll Proficiency Bonus d4s, restore that many HP (once per long rest)
Light Bearer — knows Light cantrip, Charisma is spellcasting ability
Celestial Revelation (unlocked level 3) — once per long rest, Bonus Action transform: Heavenly Wings (fly speed), Inner Radiance (radiant aura), or Necrotic Shroud (frighten nearby enemies)

narrative.appearance: Small Aasimar. Height 3'6", weight 35 lbs. Black hair, silver eyes, silver skin.
narrative.backstory: Origin unknown. Found by Ched Warmfire's original party on the road to Riverbend in the same event that brought Malachite into the group. Ched, Blair, and Rufus chose Riverbend specifically because its Halfling-founded culture of hospitality would accept two Aasimar children without question. Rufus (npc_014) became Asphodel's mentor in Riverbend.

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

party.campaign_notes: Small Aasimar. Warrior of Shadow Monk. Female. Found by Ched's party alongside Malachite (pc_002) — both Aasimar brought to Riverbend for its welcoming culture. Mentor: Rufus (npc_014). Carries +1 Brass Knuckles (item_008) — flavor name for Wraps of Unarmed Power +1. Carries Immovable Rod (item_011) — found session_006.

party.name: Derwin

species_abilities: Brave — Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition
Halfling Nimbleness — can move through the space of any creature at least one size larger
Luck — when you roll a 1 on a d20 Test, reroll and use the new result
Naturally Stealthy — can Hide even when obscured only by a creature at least one size larger

weapons: Martial weapons with Finesse or Light property

narrative.appearance: Small Halfling. Age 10, height 3ft, weight 40 lbs. Dark hair, grey eyes, light skin.
narrative.age_note: DnDB sheet shows age 10 — reflects start of campaign prologue at age 13 minus retcon, not updated on sheet. Actual age at campaign start is 18 (five years after the age-13 prologue).

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

party.campaign_notes: Halfling Rogue, Arcane Trickster. Age 18. Nephew of Ched Warmfire — both Halflings, shares the Warmfire surname. Carries Ched's Map (item_001) — passed by Blair after Ched's death, session_003. Carries Gloves of Thievery (item_007), equipped. Best friends with Goldie (pc_001) since childhood.

party.name: Atticus

species_abilities: Resourceful — gain Heroic Inspiration whenever you finish a Long Rest
Skillful — proficiency in one additional skill of choice
Versatile — gain one Origin feat of choice

milestones.note: Reached after the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Reached after the Larkspur Dragon encounter and delivery to Lake Town.

party.campaign_notes: Human Druid, Circle of Stars. Familiar: Bat. Lives primarily at his treehouse in the clearing (loc_008) rather than in Riverbend proper. The clearing is named after him. Carries +1 Moon Sickle (item_004), attuned.

narrative.summary: Prologue at age 13 featuring the first Pylon event and a wolf fight; jumps 5 years to age 18. Lifelong childhood friends remain tight-knit.

key_moments.description: First Pylon witnessed near Riverbend at age 13
Wolf fight near Atticus's Clearing — first combat
Five-year time skip to age 18 — prologue closes
Party childhood backstory established at Atticus's Clearing

narrative.opening_scene: Age 13. The party at Atticus's Clearing, familiar and easy together. Something moves in the trees.
narrative.closing_scene: Five-year time skip. The same party, older. The clearing unchanged.

player_absences.note: Ashton piloted by David due to Frazer being sick.

narrative.summary: The party is in their treehouse in Atticus's clearing when a Vespon swarm is detected in the woods. Transitioned to defending Bagyers Farm from a new Pylon emergence.
narrative.tone: High-pitched buzzing; panic; frantic defense; smell of sulfur.

key_moments.description: Vespon swarm detected in the Northern Woods near the treehouse
Party transitions to defending Bagyers Farm from a new Pylon emergence
Siege of Bagyers Farm begins — first multi-session combat
Asphodel downed for first time by Vespon Queen (Round 2)

narrative.opening_scene: The party back at the treehouse. Atticus senses something wrong in the woods before anyone else does.
narrative.closing_scene: The farm burning. The Pylon still standing. Session ends mid-siege.

mechanical_notes.note: Ashton piloted by David due to Frazer being sick.

narrative.summary: The Pylon defense concluded with the destruction of the monolith. Ched Warmfire was killed in the battle. The party resolved to set out into the world following leads from Blair and Ched's past.
narrative.tone: Somber; heavy loss; sulfurous smell of black goo.

key_moments.description: Death of Ched Warmfire
Pylon destruction by Ashton's Eldritch Blast
Discovery of Bessel's Spellbook and Ched's Map
Blair provides quests and context about Ched's past

narrative.opening_scene: Round 14 of the Siege already in progress. The Pylon is cracking.
narrative.closing_scene: Blair presses a folded map into Goli's hands without a word. The party stands in the wreckage.

player_absences.note: Malachite piloted by David due to Lance's work schedule.

narrative.summary: Escorted Carly to Menne's Ranch. Successfully negotiated a trade agreement with Helen for potion components (rewarded 50GP). Helen then requested aid with a Quarry infestation.
narrative.tone: Classic adventure feel; echoes in the subterranean dark.

key_moments.description: Escorting Carly to Menne's Ranch through the Axe Beak ambush
Trade agreement established with Helen
Quarry ROUS quest accepted from Helen
The 'Goonies Slide' — party entered the Rat Cave via water slide

narrative.opening_scene: Morning after. The party on the road with Carly's wagon, heading north.
narrative.closing_scene: The caravan forms up on the western road. A new horizon, if an uncertain one.

mechanical_notes.note: Malachite piloted by David due to Lance's work schedule.

narrative.summary: Finished the ROUS fight at the Quarry. Met travelers and agreed to escort them west to Lake Town due to goblin sightings. A goblin ambush began on the road.
narrative.tone: Tense; vigilant; smell of damp hills and goblin musk.

key_moments.description: ROUS nest cleared — cbt_004 concluded
Emerald reward received from Krustie Gnome
Stone-trading caravan met; escort contract to Lake Town accepted
Goblin ambush — cbt_005 begins, paused at end of session

narrative.opening_scene: Picking up mid-fight — cbt_004, Round 3, the Rat Cave.
narrative.closing_scene: Goblins and an Ogre in the road. Atticus's Ice Knife still smoking. The fight not done.

session_logs.title: The Larkspur Dragons

narrative.summary: Continuation of the Goblin Ambush (cbt_005). Two Larkspur dragons appeared during the fight — the party's first dragon encounter. The escorted group was killed. Party took the cart and bodies to Lake Town. Levelled up to 3. Ambushed again by goblins and ogres on the road back to Menne's Ranch. Session ends mid-combat.

session_logs.notes: PLACEHOLDER — full session data to be entered via Chronicle intake. Combat round data, key moments, and full narrative pending. Level-up milestone recorded here to anchor Phase 11 DnDBeyond sync.

session_logs.title: The Road to Belfar
session_logs.notes: PLACEHOLDER — detailed notes to follow. Full session data to be entered via Chronicle intake.

status_event.cause: Killed by Pylon spawns defending Bagyers Farm

narrative.description: A barrel-chested Halfling gone grey and hard. Smiles rarely and means it every time. Found Malachite and Asphodel on the road during his adventuring years and brought them to Riverbend, knowing they would be safe there.
narrative.personality: Bitter and protective after watching his original party decimated. Guards people he loves by pushing them away. Driven by guilt he would never name.
narrative.role_in_story: Mentor and inciting death. His loss is the emotional anchor of the first arc. His past is the mystery the party is unraveling.
narrative.significance: The party's origin point. His death in session_003 is the inciting event for qst_001.

narrative.description: Small camp presence at the Quarry. No-nonsense, directed the party to the Rat Cave without ceremony.
narrative.role_in_story: Quest facilitator — provided the lead that opened cbt_004.

narrative.description: Cleric survivor of Ched's original party. Practical, measured. Was part of the decision to bring Malachite and Asphodel to Riverbend. Gave the party their first quests without sentiment.
narrative.personality: Skilled potion brewer. Provided facts plainly and without elaboration — the absence of detail about the past is deliberate.
narrative.role_in_story: First quest giver and keeper of the party's backstory. Knows more about Ched's past than she has shared.

narrative.description: Primary contact at Menne's Ranch. Business-minded; values reliability.
narrative.role_in_story: Established the potion supply chain and directed the party to the Rat Cave infestation.

narrative.description: Caravan owner escorted to Menne's Ranch during the Axe Beak ambush in session_004.
narrative.role_in_story: One-session escort subject; no further role recorded.

status_event.cause: Killed at the same time and place as Hawk — circumstances unknown. Pre-dates campaign.

narrative.description: Wizard of Ched's original party. Known only from his spellbook — copper ink, precise hand.
narrative.role_in_story: Presence felt through item_002. His spell selection implies the party faced serious threats before being decimated.

status_event.cause: Killed at the same time and place as Bessel — circumstances unknown. Pre-dates campaign.

narrative.role_in_story: Died alongside Bessel — likely the same single catastrophic event.
Named by Blair as someone who knows about Ched's past. Marked on Ched's Map. Not yet met.
Listed on Ched's Map as a connection for jobs in Green Harbor. Not yet met.

npc_directory.name: The Low-Level Wizard

narrative.description: Traveling with the stone traders.
narrative.role_in_story: Unknown. Possibly significant given proximity to Pylon country.

npc_directory.notes: No proper name recorded as of session_005.

npc_directory.name: Rufus

narrative.description: Bald male Monk. One of three survivors of Ched's original party. Returned to Riverbend with Ched and Blair after the decimation. Chose to stay and became a mentor figure in the community — specifically took on Asphodel (pc_004) as a student.
narrative.role_in_story: Mentor to Asphodel. A living witness to whatever destroyed the original party — has not been approached yet.

npc_directory.notes: Has not appeared on-screen as of session_005 despite being in Riverbend since session_001. An unexploited lead.

observed_abilities.name: Pylon-spawned
observed_abilities.description: Emerged from Pylon during the Siege of Bagyers Farm.

observed_abilities.name: Sulfurous residue
observed_abilities.description: Emits sulfur smell; leaves black goo on death.

observed_weaknesses: Destroying the Pylon source stops spawning

narrative.description: A crustacean-form creature spawned by the Pylon — not a natural animal. Hard-shelled, clawed, moves with mechanical purpose.
narrative.encounter_notes: Spawned continuously from the Pylon during cbt_002. Eliminating the Pylon source stopped the spawning. Attacking individual creatures was attrition; destroying the Pylon was the solution.

observed_abilities.name: Aggressive charge
observed_abilities.description: Charges targets directly; prioritised horses over party members during cbt_003.

observed_abilities.name: Large flightless bird
observed_abilities.description: Significant size — able to trample and knock prone.

narrative.description: Large flightless bird with a razor-edged beak and powerful legs. Territorial and aggressive.
narrative.encounter_notes: Prioritised horses over party members — tactical consideration for future encounters with mounts or large animals nearby. Vespon Swarm was present simultaneously in cbt_003.

observed_abilities.name: Swarm coordination
observed_abilities.description: Controlled by the Vespon Queen. Lost formation and became stationary when Queen was incapacitated.

observed_abilities.name: Sleep vulnerability
observed_abilities.description: Multiple swarm members eliminated by a single Sleep spell in cbt_001.

observed_abilities.name: Mold Earth weakness
observed_abilities.description: Entombed via Mold Earth in the cbt_001 finale — unconventional but effective.

observed_weaknesses: Sleep spell (multiple members affected by one casting)
Loses coordination when Queen is incapacitated

narrative.description: A churning cloud of insect-sized creatures moving with unsettling coordination. Individual creatures are small; the mass is not.
narrative.encounter_notes: The key discovery: swarm loses coordination when the Queen is taken out. Swarm alone is manageable; swarm with active Queen is not. Sleep and AoE spells effective.

observed_abilities.name: Cycle of boldness and retreat
observed_abilities.description: Advance aggressively then fall back when the tactical situation shifts.

observed_abilities.name: Coordinated with slingers
observed_abilities.description: Operated alongside Goblin Slingers and an Ogre in cbt_005 — suggests organised force.

narrative.description: Standard goblin — small, quick, cowardly alone, dangerous in numbers. Uses scrub and darkness.
narrative.encounter_notes: Organised force — not an opportunistic raid. Slingers, standard goblins, and an Ogre in deliberate formation. Suggests a commander or at least a plan.

observed_abilities.name: Sonic Buzz
observed_abilities.description: Special attack. Used on Asphodel in Round 2 of cbt_001 — downed immediately (12 damage).

observed_abilities.name: Swarm command
observed_abilities.description: Commands Vespon Swarms directly. Swarm loses coordination when Queen is incapacitated.

observed_abilities.name: Killed by Asphodel
observed_abilities.description: Eliminated in Round 4 of cbt_001 by Asphodel.

observed_weaknesses: Unarmed strikes (killed by Asphodel, Round 4 cbt_001)

narrative.description: Larger than the swarm members. A distinct creature that commands rather than constitutes the swarm.
narrative.encounter_notes: Priority target in any Vespon engagement. Sonic Buzz is the dangerous ability — single-target but high damage. Killed by Asphodel using Unarmed Strike in Round 4.

observed_abilities.name: Ranged sling attacks
observed_abilities.description: Operates at range while standard goblins engage in melee.

observed_abilities.name: Coordinated positioning
observed_abilities.description: Stayed back during goblin advance, providing ranged support.

narrative.description: A goblin variant that hangs back and uses a sling. Distinguishable by positioning and attack pattern rather than appearance.
narrative.encounter_notes: Remove or close distance on slingers early. They are low-damage but persistent — the accumulated hits from Del being targeted multiple times contributed to two downed events.

observed_abilities.name: Delayed entry
observed_abilities.description: Appeared at the treeline at the end of Round 1 — not visible until the ambush was already underway.

observed_abilities.name: High melee damage
observed_abilities.description: Dealt 12 damage to Malachite in Round 3, 10 damage in Round 2. Greatclub-type attacks.

observed_abilities.name: Killed by Ice Knife
observed_abilities.description: Eliminated by Atticus (pc_006) via Ice Knife in Round 3 of cbt_005. Shard burst hit Malachite but he saved.

observed_weaknesses: Ice Knife (killed by Atticus, Round 3 cbt_005)

narrative.description: Standard ogre. Large, slow to arrive, dangerous once engaged. Appeared as a second wave behind the goblin advance.
narrative.encounter_notes: Arrived late but escalated the fight significantly. Ice Knife was the killing blow; the shard burst was a near-miss for Malachite. Engagement confirmed: Ogres take positioning seriously — appeared only after goblins were established.

observed_abilities.name: Pack creature
observed_abilities.description: Encountered in large numbers in the cavern. Coordinated pack attacks across multiple targets.

observed_abilities.name: ROUS designation
observed_abilities.description: Referred to in-game as ROUS (Rodents of Unusual Size) — table nickname that stuck.

observed_abilities.name: Endurance
observed_abilities.description: Infestation required 7 rounds across 2 sessions to clear completely.

narrative.description: Giant rats — larger than expected, aggressive when cornered. Pack mentality. The 'ROUS' designation emerged at the table and stuck.
narrative.encounter_notes: Cave environment was a factor — tight corridors limited AoE options. The water slide entry (no choice on positioning) made the opening round difficult.

mechanics.verifiable_fact: No record of Pylon structures exists in any chronicle prior to approximately 5 years ago.

narrative.content: No history of Pylons exists in Goldie's chronicles prior to 5 years ago.
narrative.implications: Either the Pylons are genuinely new to the world, or every record of their prior existence has been removed. Goldie considers the second possibility more troubling than the first.
narrative.voice: Framed through Goldie's scholarly perspective — he is the party's expert on historical records and the absence of Pylon history in any source is the detail that unsettles him most.

mechanics.verifiable_fact: Ched's original party of five was decimated: Bessel (Wizard) and Hawk (Ranger) were killed; Ched (Fighter), Blair (Cleric), and Rufus (Monk) survived and returned to Riverbend.

narrative.content: Consisted of Ched (Fighter), Blair (Cleric), Bessel (Wizard), Hawk (Ranger), and Rufus (Monk). Bessel and Hawk were killed. Ched, Blair, and Rufus survived and returned to Riverbend.
narrative.implications: Bessel and Hawk died together — the circumstances are unknown. Ched, Blair, and Rufus returned but none will speak freely about what happened. Blair provided the bare facts in session_003. Rufus is in Riverbend and has not been spoken to. The parallel deaths of Bessel and Hawk suggest a single catastrophic event rather than attrition over time.
narrative.voice: Established through Blair's account in session_003. Blair gave the facts plainly and without elaboration — the absence of detail is as significant as what was said.

narrative.content: Riverbend was founded by Halflings within the past few generations as a rural farming community and waypoint on the road between Belfar and Green Harbor. Its founding principles are explicitly those of Halfling hospitality: openness, welcome, and a community defined by who chooses to settle rather than by ancestry or claim. The town is diverse precisely because it was designed to be — people came because the road brought them through, and they stayed because they were made welcome. There is no original or ancestral group who considers themselves native; the identity of Riverbend is the choice to be here. This is why Ched Warmfire, Blair, and Rufus brought Malachite and Asphodel — two Aasimar children whose celestial nature made them unwelcome elsewhere — to Riverbend specifically. They knew the town would not question them.
narrative.thematic_significance: Riverbend is the opposite of the world that cast Malachite out. A place founded on the principle that who you are when you arrive matters more than where you came from. The party grew up here — their tolerance, their instinct to include, their found-family dynamic all trace back to this founding culture.

mechanics.provenance: Belonged to Ched Warmfire. Passed to Goli at the scene of his death, session_003. Ched had been keeping and adding to it since the decimation of his original party.

narrative.description: A folded parchment, well-worn at the creases. Five names in Ched's cramped hand — each one a thread he never got to pull.
narrative.significance: The party's primary navigational lead for the Ched investigation. The unknown markers (? and X) suggest Ched was tracking something he hadn't yet identified.
narrative.condition: Intact. Slightly singed at one corner.

inventory_and_loot.name: Bessel's Spellbook

mechanics.provenance: Belonged to Bessel (npc_009), Wizard of Ched's original party. Recovered from the Bagyers Farm site during session_003.

narrative.description: A leather-bound spellbook. Bessel's name scratched inside the front cover in a precise copper-ink hand that has not faded.
narrative.significance: Connects the party to Ched's original party. The spell selection — Fireball and Tiny Hut among the higher entries — implies Bessel prepared for serious threats.
narrative.condition: Intact and legible despite the circumstances of recovery.

inventory_and_loot.notes: Goldie transcribed all 7 level-1 spells from this book after session_003 (see mechanics.contents.spells for full list). Level-2 spells (Knock, Misty Step, Scorching Ray, Spider Climb) and level-3 spells (Fireball, Tiny Hut) remain in the book only — not transcribed into Goldie's personal spellbook. Note: Knock, Misty Step, Scorching Ray, Spider Climb appear in Goldie's spellbook in DnDB — the DM may have allowed full transcription. Confirm.

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. 4 potions recovered.

narrative.description: Four standard red-stoppered potions of healing.
narrative.significance: Party consumable reserve. Carried by Goli.

inventory_and_loot.name: +1 Moon Sickle

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. Previous owner unknown.

narrative.description: A silver-bladed sickle that glimmers softly with moonlight. +1 to attack and damage rolls, +1 to druid/ranger spell attack rolls and save DCs. Can be used as a spellcasting focus. When casting a healing spell while holding it, roll a d4 and add to HP restored.
narrative.significance: Primary weapon for Atticus. The moon motif aligns with his druidic practice.

inventory_and_loot.notes: Requires attunement by a druid or ranger. Atticus has it attuned and equipped. DnDB item name: Moon Sickle, +1.

inventory_and_loot.name: Boots of Striding and Springing

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. Previous owner unknown.

narrative.description: Sturdy boots with spring-coiled runes at the ankle. Speed becomes 30ft (unless already higher) and is not reduced by encumbrance or heavy armour. Once per turn, jump up to 30ft by spending only 10ft of movement.
narrative.significance: Carried by Malachite — suits the Barbarian's mobile combat style.

inventory_and_loot.notes: Requires attunement. Attuned and equipped by Malachite. DnDB item name: Boots of Striding and Springing.

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. Previous owner unknown.

narrative.description: A deep grey cloak that seems to deflect harm as much as weather.
narrative.significance: Carried by Ashton.

inventory_and_loot.name: Gloves of Thievery

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. Previous owner unknown.

narrative.description: Thin leather gloves, invisible while worn. +5 bonus to Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) checks and Dexterity checks to pick locks.
narrative.significance: Carried by Goli — complements his Rogue toolkit.

inventory_and_loot.notes: No attunement required. Equipped by Goli.

inventory_and_loot.name: +1 Brass Knuckles

mechanics.provenance: Found in an adventuring chest at Bagyers Farm, session_003. Previous owner unknown.

narrative.description: Brass-plated hand wraps enchanted with a +1 bonus. Mechanically equivalent to Wraps of Unarmed Power +1 (DnD Beyond). "Brass Knuckles" is the table's flavor name for this item.
narrative.significance: Primary weapon for Asphodel — well suited to a Monk's unarmed combat focus.

inventory_and_loot.notes: DnD Beyond equivalent: Wraps of Unarmed Power +1. "+1 Brass Knuckles" is flavor — not an official item name. Grants +1 to unarmed attack and damage rolls; damage is Force or normal type.

mechanics.provenance: 30 GP commission paid by Helen at Menne's Ranch upon completion of the Potion Supply Chain quest, session_004.

narrative.description: 30 GP trade commission. Held as party funds.
narrative.significance: First significant monetary reward. Reward for qst_004.

mechanics.provenance: Reward from Helen at Menne's Ranch for clearing the ROUS infestation (qst_005). Received session_005. Valued at approximately 100 GP unrefined.

narrative.description: Reward for ROUS cave. Valued at 100 GP.
narrative.significance: Reward for qst_005. Most valuable single item in the party's possession.
narrative.condition: Unrefined — requires a jeweller to assess final value.

inventory_and_loot.name: Immovable Rod

mechanics.provenance: Loot from session_006 — session not yet logged in Chronicle.

narrative.description: An iron rod with a button on one end. Pressing the button fixes the rod magically in place until the button is pressed again. Holds up to 8,000 lbs. DC 30 Strength check to move it 10 feet.
narrative.significance: Utility item with creative combat and exploration applications.

inventory_and_loot.notes: Found during session_006 — full loot context pending Chronicle intake of that session. Currently carried by Asphodel (pc_004).

narrative.description: A rural farming community and waypoint on the road between Belfar (loc_006) and Green Harbor (loc_012), situated on bluffs above the Merelane River. Founded by Halflings within the past few generations as a place of hospitality and passage — diverse and welcoming by founding principle, not by historical accident. The town grew as people chose to settle here, not because a particular group considered themselves native to the land. Contains the Warmfire Inn (owned by Goli's parents) and a general store (owned by Ashton's parents). Goldie's family settled here as immigrants escaping a war in their homelands. Atticus lives mostly at his treehouse in the clearing (loc_008).
narrative.significance: Hometown of the entire party and the anchor of their shared identity. Halfling-founded culture of hospitality made it the right place for Ched to bring two Aasimar children — Malachite and Asphodel — knowing they would be accepted without question. Ched, Blair, and Rufus all live here. Goli's family inn (Warmfire Inn) is a social anchor for the community.
narrative.founding: Founded by Halflings within the past few generations. Built on principles of hospitality and openness — the town's identity is defined by who chose to settle here, not by any original or ancestral claim. Deliberately diverse. The road position between Belfar and Green Harbor ensured steady traffic and cultural mixing from the start.

narrative.description: Dwarven and Gnomish mining site, approximately 2 days southwest of Menne's Ranch. A camp of temporary tents and few permanent structures for workers.
narrative.significance: Staging point for the Rat Cave quest. Krustie Gnome directed the party here. Site of cbt_005 (Goblin Ambush) on the road approaching.

narrative.description: A working farm on the bluff road out of Riverbend. The eastern field is still black and sulfurous where the Pylon monolith stood.
narrative.significance: Site of Ched's death and the first major Pylon encounter. The campaign's emotional ground zero.

narrative.description: Horse-farming community north of Riverbend. Prime horse country with a Main Street featuring a Tavern and Shop.
narrative.significance: Helen's base of operations. The party established the potion supply chain here and received directions to the Quarry ROUS problem.

narrative.description: Infested cavern near The Quarry. Accessed via a water slide into a dark, echoing chamber.
narrative.significance: Site of cbt_004 — the ROUS infestation the party was hired to clear. The 'Goonies slide' entry became a memorable session moment.

narrative.description: City to the northeast. First major destination on Ched's Map.
narrative.significance: Primary destination for Pylon research and the Ched investigation. Darnell (npc_011) is here. The party has not arrived yet.

narrative.description: Dense forest extending north of Riverbend. The party's childhood exploration grounds. Atticus's Clearing (loc_008) lies within these woods — Atticus's familiarity with this area gave early warning of both the Wolf Fight (cbt_006) and the Vespon Queen encounter (cbt_001).
narrative.significance: Site of cbt_001 (Vespon Queen) and cbt_006 (Wolf Fight), both near Atticus's Clearing. Contains loc_008 as a sub-location. Atticus's nature connection to this area is an established campaign mechanic.

narrative.description: A specific grove within the Northern Woods where Atticus grew up. Contains the party's childhood treehouse base — their headquarters for make-believe adventures and, eventually, real ones. Atticus lives here rather than in Riverbend proper.
narrative.significance: The found-family origin point referenced in moment_003. Where the party's shared history is literally rooted. Atticus's deep connection to this part of the woods means he senses threats in the surrounding area — both cbt_001 (Vespon Queen) and cbt_006 (Wolf Fight) originated nearby, with Atticus aware of the danger before the party encountered it.
Marked on Ched's Map beside the name 'Fish'. No other context recorded.
Marked with '?' on Ched's Map. Location and significance unknown.

mechanics.region: Approx. 2 days west of The Quarry

narrative.description: Approximately 2 days west of The Quarry. Current temporary destination.
narrative.significance: The party's immediate destination as of session_005. Escort contract for the stone traders ends here.

narrative.description: Major port city to the south.
narrative.significance: Kiki (npc_012) is based here. Marked on Ched's Map. Not yet visited.
Marked with 'X' on Ched's Map. Location and significance unknown.

objectives.description: Find Darnell in Belfar to learn about Ched's past.

progress_log.fact: Ched Warmfire killed by Pylon spawns during the Siege of Bagyers Farm.
Blair suggested finding Darnell in Belfar. Darnell is marked on Ched's Map.

narrative.motivation: Ched's death demands retribution — but retribution requires understanding. Who was Ched before Riverbend? What decimated his original party? The map in Goli's hands suggests he was still looking for answers.
narrative.thematic_weight: The party's first personal quest. Grief expressed as purpose.
open_questions: What happened to Ched's original party?
What does Darnell know that Ched never shared?
Is the Pylon anomaly connected to what destroyed the original party?
What happened to Rufus — he returned to Riverbend but has never been spoken to on-screen.

objectives.description: Seek information in Belfar about the Pylons.

progress_log.fact: First Pylon structure witnessed by the group near Riverbend.
Bagyers Farm Pylon neutralized via Ashton's Eldritch Blast. Pylon phenomenon remains active elsewhere.

narrative.motivation: The Pylons appeared without precedent five years ago. Goldie's research confirms no historical record of their existence. Something is wrong with the world and the party witnessed it firsthand.
narrative.thematic_weight: The campaign's central mystery. Everything else orbits this.
open_questions: What are the Pylons and where did they come from?
Why do they spawn hostile creatures?
Is the timing of their appearance — five years ago — connected to the decimation of Ched's party?

reward_received.description: 36 GP contract payment (6 GP/person × 6 party members) from Lake Town upon delivery.

objectives.description: Escort the stone-trading caravan to Lake Town.

progress_log.fact: Accepted 6 GP/person contract to escort stone-trading caravan to Lake Town.
Ambushed by goblins and an Ogre mid-route. Combat paused at end of session_005.
Two Larkspur dragons appeared during the goblin ambush. The escorted group was killed in the fighting.
Party delivered the stone cargo and the bodies of the escorted group to Lake Town. Contract fulfilled despite the loss of the clients.
Lake Town paid the 36 GP contract. Town mourned the dead but was grateful to have them returned.

narrative.motivation: A contract job — six gold per person to escort stone traders to Lake Town. Simple, until it wasn't.
narrative.thematic_weight: A contract fulfilled in the worst possible way. The party succeeded by the letter of the agreement and failed by every other measure. Lake Town paid; the traders are buried there. The party took the cart.
open_questions: What is the Low-Level Wizard's (npc_013) story — were they among those killed?
Does the failure to protect the clients carry any reputation consequence on the road?

reward_received.description: 30 GP trade commission from Helen at Menne's Ranch

objectives.description: Establish trade agreement with Helen at Menne's Ranch.

progress_log.fact: Escorted Carly (npc_008) to Menne's Ranch and established trade agreement with Helen (npc_007).

narrative.motivation: Blair needed a supply chain established. The party needed income and direction. Simple alignment of interests.
narrative.thematic_weight: First completed quest. Established the party as capable of following through on obligations.

reward_received.description: Unrefined emerald from Krustie Gnome at The Quarry as payment for clearing the ROUS infestation.

objectives.description: Clear the ROUS infestation from the cave near The Quarry.

progress_log.fact: Received directions from Krustie (npc_003). Discovered the Rat Cave via water slide entry.
Cleared the ROUS nest across cbt_004. Provided tails to Krustie Gnome as proof.
Unrefined emerald (item_010, ~100 GP) received from Krustie Gnome (npc_003) at The Quarry.

narrative.motivation: Helen needed an infestation cleared. The party needed coin.
narrative.thematic_weight: A clean job well done. The 'Goonies slide' entry became the session's most memorable moment.

enemies_roster.notes: Put to sleep by pc_001 Sleep in Round 4. Entombed by Mold Earth finale.

pcs_downed.notes: Downed by Vespon Queen Sonic Buzz (12 dmg). Revived by party before Round 3.

enemies_eliminated.notes: Sleep used; entombed by Mold Earth — not individually killed

narrative.setup: Encounter in the Northern Woods near Atticus's Clearing. Atticus sensed the swarm before it was visible.

turning_point.description: Goldie cast Sleep on the swarm; Atti used Mold Earth to entomb them while incapacitated. Unconventional but decisive.

narrative.finale: Atti and Goldie used Mold Earth to entomb the sleeping swarm.

special_events.description: pc_004 downed by Sonic Buzz (12 dmg)

rounds.summary: Queen killed; remaining swarm put to sleep and entombed via Mold Earth.

combat_encounters.name: Siege of Bagyers Farm

enemies_roster.notes: Pylon-spawned. Continuous reinforcements until Pylon destroyed Round 11.

pcs_downed.notes: Del at 1HP Round 6, then DOWNED Round 7. Death saves Rounds 7-11.
DOWNED Round 7. Death saves Rounds 8-11. Stabilised Round 10.
Ash DOWNED Round 12. Death saves Rounds 13-16.
Att DOWNED Round 14. Death saves Rounds 15-17. Stabilised Round 17.

enemies_eliminated.notes: Ongoing kills throughout. Final Rock Lobster killed Round 19.
Destroyed by Eldritch Blast — stopped Rock Lobster spawning.

narrative.setup: Sulfur smell preceded the Pylon emergence. Rock Lobsters spawned continuously from the monolith. Ched Warmfire fought alongside the party.

turning_point.description: Ashton's Eldritch Blast destroyed the Pylon. Rock Lobster spawning stopped immediately. The remaining creatures were mopped up over 8 more rounds.

narrative.finale: The party barely survived the 19-round siege. Ched Warmfire was killed in the chaos. Loot recovered from a chest in the aftermath.

enemy_turns.notes: Hits on Goli, Del, Mal — minor damage

rounds.summary: Pylon collapses and begins to dissolve; spawns continue fighting.
Victory. Pylon neutralized. All spawns defeated. Ched Warmfire confirmed dead.

combat_encounters.notes: ★ Ched fell during this encounter. Pylon destroyed Rd 11.

enemies_roster.notes: Road ambush. Killed both party horses.
Last Vespon killed by pc_002 in Round 11.

pcs_downed.notes: Del DOWNED Round 7 by Axe Beak attack.
Ash DOWNED Round 9. Nat 20 death save Round 10 — back to 1HP.

narrative.setup: Road ambush while escorting Carly to Menne's Ranch. Axe Beaks emerged first, then Vespon Swarm. Both party horses were killed.

turning_point.description: Both horses killed in the same round Del was downed. The encounter shifted from escort to survival.

narrative.aftermath: Del, Atticus, and Goldie used Wild Shape and familiars to turn into/summon goats to pull the wagon the rest of the way to Menne's Ranch.

enemy_turns.notes: 1 swarm attacks each horse (2 total). Del hit.

action.name: Sleep
action.notes: 2 Vespon failed save; wake up after.

enemies_roster.notes: Final ROUS killed by pc_003 Eldritch Blast in Round 7.

enemies_eliminated.notes: Final ROUS. Multiple kills across all 7 rounds.

narrative.setup: Party entered via water slide into a dark echoing cavern. No preparation time — ROUS were present on arrival.

turning_point.description: Malachite's SPLAT critical hit shifted the momentum. Table responded audibly. Pack coordination began to collapse.

enemy_turns.notes: Heavy damage round — pack attacks across all targets

enemies_roster.notes: Several killed. Count unknown. Combat paused.
Appeared at treeline end of Round 1. Killed by pc_006 Ice Knife in Round 3.

pcs_downed.notes: Del DOWNED by Goblin Slinger sling. Revived by pc_006 Cure Wounds same round.

narrative.setup: Ambush from roadside scrub west of the Quarry. Goblins first, Ogre appeared at treeline end of Round 1.

turning_point.description: Atticus's Ice Knife dropped the Ogre. Shard burst nearly caught Malachite but he saved. Goblins lost their anchor.

narrative.current_status: Paused at end of Round 3. Remaining goblins still active. Continues session_006.

special_events.description: Ogre emerged from treeline at end of Round 1

enemy_turns.notes: No attack this round — appeared only

action.name: Ice Knife
action.notes: ★ Ogre killed; Mal saved vs burst

special_events.description: Ogre killed by pc_006 Ice Knife (slot 3) this round

combat_encounters.name: Wolf Fight

narrative.setup: Prologue combat at age 13, near Atticus's Clearing in the Northern Woods. The party's first encounter as a group.

combat_encounters.notes: Round-by-round details pending. Referenced in session_001 summary.

mechanics.mechanical_action: Atticus (pc_006) cast Cure Wounds on downed Asphodel (pc_004) as the Action in slot 3 of Round 2, cbt_005 — before any other party member had acted after Del was dropped.

narrative.description: Atticus healed Asphodel immediately after they were downed by a flurry of goblin/ogre attacks, keeping the frontline from collapsing.
narrative.significance: Established the Atticus-Del support dynamic. Atticus's response was instinctive rather than tactical — no hesitation, no target assessment. Del barely registered what happened before they were back on their feet.

mechanics.mechanical_action: Malachite (pc_002) landed a critical hit on a ROUS during cbt_004 (The Rat Cave). The result was described at the table as a 'SPLAT!' that set the tone for the encounter's conclusion.

narrative.description: Malachite landed a devastating critical hit on a ROUS, described narratively as a 'SPLAT!' that set the tone for the cleanup.
narrative.significance: One of Mal's defining combat moments — not just the damage, but the way the table responded to it. Established Malachite as capable of violence that is both effective and viscerally satisfying.

mechanics.mechanical_action: Non-combat. During the session_001 prologue, the party established their shared childhood history at Atticus's Clearing — the treehouse base used for make-believe adventures at age 13.

narrative.description: The party shares a childhood history of playing at 'Atticus's Clearing,' having utilized an expanded treehouse as their base for make-believe adventures long before becoming actual adventurers.
narrative.significance: The found-family foundation of the campaign. Every subsequent moment of mutual sacrifice or loyalty traces back to this shared origin. The clearing itself becomes a meaningful location rather than a neutral staging area.

narrative.premise: Six lifelong childhood friends from the village of Riverbend ventured out following the death of their mentor Ched Warmfire — killed defending Bagyers Farm from a Pylon emergence. Armed with fragments of his unresolved past, a map pointing to people he never reached, and loot recovered from the wreckage, they stepped into a world they had only ever read about.
narrative.current_arc: Traveling west toward Lake Town to fulfill a 6 GP/person escort contract and earn funds before pivoting northeast to Belfar — the first destination on Ched's Map and the party's best lead on the Pylon mystery.
narrative.tone: Classic adventure with escalating stakes; loss as emotional anchor; found-family dynamics over dungeon mechanics.

ruleset.summary: Content replaced in 5.5e (2024) uses 5.5e rules. Content not replaced in 5.5e remains available under original 5e text. All 5e expanded content is available unless it has been rewritten in 5.5e.
ruleset.priority_rule: Where a rule or feature exists in both 5e and 5.5e, the 5.5e version takes precedence. Where only a 5e version exists (not rewritten in 5.5e), the 5e text applies. Expanded content from both editions is available on the same basis.
known_implications: Warlock Pact Boon replaced by Eldritch Invocations in 5.5e — invocations used (e.g. Pact of the Blade is now an invocation)
Agonizing Blast (5.5e) now requires cantrip selection at time of invocation choice
Species traits use 5.5e text where the species appears in PHB 2024
Background feat granted at level 1 follows 5.5e structure
Class feature names and levels may differ from 5e equivalents — 5.5e names used where applicable
ruleset.notes: DnDBeyond character sheets are built using 5.5e rules with backwards compatibility enabled. The ddb_synced field on each party member records when the sheet was last imported. Any discrepancies between 5e and 5.5e text should resolve in favour of 5.5e unless the DM has explicitly ruled otherwise.
